Mtn For Order Quashing Subpoenas, Granting A Protective Order & Requiring An In Camera Inspection Of Docs
Off Calendar Dec-27-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Herbert Yanowitz, appeared for Plaintiff/moving party Herbert S. Kaufman
Ricahrd Grabstein, appeared for Defendant/Opposing party Richard H. Kaufman
Candace Holroyd, CSR #1370

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion For Order Quashing Subpoenas, Granting A Protective Order Against Disclosure Of Documents Sought By Defendant, and Requiring An In Camera Inspection Of Documents Sought By Defendant is GRANTED as it seeks to quash the subpoenas. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Ntc Of Mtn Compelling (Further) Responses To Inspection Demands, And Compelling Production Of Documents For Inspection And Copying And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-27-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Richard Grabstein, appeared for Defendant/moving party Richard H. Kaufman
Herbert Yanotwitz, appeared for Plaintiff/Opposing party Herbert S. Kaufman
Candace Holroyd, CSR #1370

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demands, and Compelling Production Of Documents For Inspection and Copying is GRANTED in part and denied in part.
As to No. 1, Plaintiff is ordered to produce as indicated in the response. As to the police report, redact any communications for which a privilege is claimed.
As to No. 2, Plaintiff is ordered to produce that portion of the trust agreement that evidences an intent to transfer ownership of the firearms.
As to Nos. 3 and 4, the motion is granted, limited to those portions which designate Mr. Yanowitz as a successor co-trustee and executor.
As to No. 6, the motion is denied.
As to Nos. 7 and 8, the motion is denied. The responses are adequate.
As to No. 11, Plaintiff is ordered to respond further, producing documents and identifying with particularity any documents withheld pursuant to privilege.
As to No. 14, Plaintiff is ordered to respond further, identifying responsive documents which have been produced and identifying with particularity any documents withheld pursuant to privilege.
Further responses and production shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
